page: Google Cloud Platform m4-hypermem-64 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the m4 server family. It features an x86_64 CPU architecture with 64 dedicated virtual CPUs (vCPUs) and 992.0 GB of system memory. This configuration yields a high resource density of 15.5 GB of RAM per vCPU core, making it suitable for tasks requiring substantial memory footprints relative to compute capacity.

This instance does not include integrated local storage or bundled GPU hardware, meaning all storage requirements must be met via external network-attached block storage. Additionally, the instance does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses, requiring standard networking configurations for external connectivity.

Given the high memory-to-vCPU ratio, the m4-hypermem-64 is designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for applications requiring massive memory capacity without the cost of additional unneeded compute cores, such as large-scale in-memory databases, real-time data analytics, and enterprise resource planning systems.
description: Google Cloud Platform m4-hypermem-64 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance from the m4 family, utilizing the x86_64 CPU architecture. The hardware configuration delivers 64 dedicated vCPUs and 992.0 GB of system memory, establishing a high resource density of 15.5 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not feature local storage or integrated GPU hardware, meaning all storage needs must be handled via network-attached volumes. The m4-hypermem-64 is qualitatively cost-efficient for enterprise environments that require massive memory capacity without the overhead of excessive compute cores. It is suited for workloads such as large-scale in-memory databases, real-time data analytics pipelines, and enterprise resource planning applications.
